senses.gloss: wild-ass, onager, especially a young male

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *faraʾ-, related to the root ف ر ر (f r r) meaning "to be escaped", "to flee", "to be hasty or fleet", "to be hard to catch", used also describe the young of other animals like sheep, goats, wild cows, gazelle, antelope. Compare Akkadian 𒉺𒊏𒌑 (parû), Hebrew פֶּרֶא (pére, “wild ass”), Somali faruw (“zebra”), Chinese 福祿 /福禄 (fúlù, “zebra”).
